note: Standard adult reference ranges for common panels. Flags are informational only — never a diagnosis. 'explains' is plain-language education, not medical advice.

hba1c.name: HbA1c
hba1c.explains: Your average blood sugar over the past 2-3 months. Higher means a greater risk of diabetes.

fasting_glucose.name: Fasting Glucose
fasting_glucose.explains: Blood sugar after fasting. A snapshot of how your body handles glucose.

total_cholesterol.name: Total Cholesterol
total_cholesterol.explains: All the cholesterol in your blood. High levels raise heart-disease risk over time.

ldl.name: LDL Cholesterol
ldl.explains: The 'bad' cholesterol that builds up in arteries. Lower is better.

hdl.name: HDL Cholesterol
hdl.explains: The 'good' cholesterol that clears arteries. Here, higher is better.

triglycerides.name: Triglycerides
triglycerides.explains: A fat in your blood. High levels often track with diet, weight and heart risk.

systolic_bp.name: Blood Pressure (Systolic)
systolic_bp.explains: The top blood-pressure number — pressure when your heart beats.

diastolic_bp.name: Blood Pressure (Diastolic)
diastolic_bp.explains: The bottom blood-pressure number — pressure when your heart rests.

creatinine.name: Creatinine
creatinine.explains: A waste product your kidneys filter. High levels can signal reduced kidney function.

egfr.name: eGFR
egfr.explains: How well your kidneys filter blood. Lower means weaker kidney function.

uric_acid.name: Uric Acid
uric_acid.explains: A waste product; high levels can cause gout and kidney stones.

alt.name: ALT (SGPT)
alt.explains: A liver enzyme. Raised levels can indicate liver stress or inflammation.

ast.name: AST (SGOT)
ast.explains: A liver and muscle enzyme. Often checked alongside ALT for liver health.

hemoglobin.name: Hemoglobin
hemoglobin.explains: The protein carrying oxygen in your blood. Low means anaemia; high can mean dehydration.

vitamin_d.name: Vitamin D (25-OH)
vitamin_d.explains: Vitamin D level. Low is very common and affects bones and immunity.

tsh.name: TSH
tsh.explains: Thyroid signal hormone. High suggests an underactive thyroid, low an overactive one.

hematocrit.name: Hematocrit
hematocrit.explains: The share of your blood made of red cells. Low can mean anaemia, high dehydration.

rbc.name: Red Blood Cells (Erythrocytes)
rbc.explains: The cells that carry oxygen. Counted to screen for anaemia or other blood conditions.

mcv.name: MCV (Red Cell Size)
mcv.explains: The average size of your red blood cells — helps classify the type of anaemia.

mch.name: MCH (Hemoglobin per Cell)
mch.explains: Average amount of oxygen-carrying protein per red cell.

mchc.name: MCHC (Hemoglobin Concentration)
mchc.explains: How concentrated the hemoglobin is inside red cells.

platelets.name: Platelets (Thrombocytes)
platelets.explains: Cells that help blood clot. Low raises bleeding risk; very high can raise clotting risk — relevant in dengue too.

wbc.name: White Blood Cells (Leukocytes)
wbc.explains: Your immune cells. High often means infection; low can mean a weakened immune system.

neutrophils.name: Neutrophils
neutrophils.explains: The immune cells that fight bacterial infection. The largest share of white cells.

lymphocytes.name: Lymphocytes
lymphocytes.explains: Immune cells that fight viruses and build long-term immunity.

monocytes.name: Monocytes
monocytes.explains: Immune cells that clean up infection and debris.

eosinophils.name: Eosinophils
eosinophils.explains: Immune cells linked to allergies and parasitic infections.

basophils.name: Basophils
basophils.explains: The rarest white cells, involved in allergic reactions.
